commit.message:
Redesign board outline to follow mansion shape with decorative details

Remove the rectangular red border around the board. Instead, each cell
computes which edges face empty/wall space and renders per-edge borders
that trace the actual mansion floor plan. Empty wall cells are now
transparent, revealing the page background.

Adds garden greenery (radial gradient shrubs) on exterior wall cells
adjacent to the mansion, character-colored splashes on starting positions,
and a directional outer glow on the mansion edge. All decorations are
theme-aware and hidden in vintage mode.

fix: block direct pushes to main, release uses PR for version bumps (#12)

- Ruleset bypass changed from "always" to "pull_request" mode, preventing
  all direct pushes to main (including from admins)
- Release workflow now creates a PR for CHANGELOG.md and package.json
  updates instead of pushing directly to main
- Tag push changed from --tags to specific tag for safety
- Setup guide updated to document rulesets (not legacy branch protection)
  and the new bypass mode

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

WIP: dual-geom visibility pattern for task objects, cleanup rotation hack

Split task object geoms (cube, pencil, screwdriver) into collision +
visual pairs for viser rendering. Remove broken init_rot workaround
code from RobotCfg. Update README with multi-hand instructions.

KNOWN BROKEN:
- xhand meshes invisible in viser (0 visual geoms, needs dual-geom)
- Hand orientation not palm-up (mjlab ignores init_rot for fixed-base)
- Possible duplicate object rendering in viser

See .claude/docs/fix_pose_debug.md for investigation notes and fix plan.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Distinguish separate events in SMS answers: dates, day-of-week, event separation

- Include day-of-week + short date on each fact (e.g. "sent Wednesday Mar 4")
  so LLM resolves "today" relative to when the message was sent, not query time
- Tell LLM today's actual date for temporal reasoning
- Explicitly instruct LLM that different event names (formal, PFC, mixer)
  are SEPARATE events — do not combine their details

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Union detail page Loading hang: broken profiles FK join + resilient presence

The getUnionClubs query used profiles:owner_id(display_name) which resolved
through clubs.owner_id FK to auth.users (not profiles), causing a 42703 error
that crashed loadData and left the page stuck on Loading forever.

Fixes:
- Remove broken nested profiles join from getUnionClubs and getAdmins
- Fetch profile names via separate query to profiles table
- Wrap each enrichment query in try/catch so one failure doesn't block all
- Add 5s timeout to presence getUser() to prevent hang
- Fix getMyUnions to fetch admin union data separately (was using removed join)
- Fix updateClubSplits to use actual column club_commission_rate

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: add macOS bootstrap and cross-platform improvements

Add self-contained bootstrap/macos.sh (8 idempotent phases). On macOS,
dev tools from [container].packages are installed directly via nix profile
from the same flake that builds the Linux distrobox image, giving tool
parity without distrobox.

Key changes:
- bootstrap/macos.sh: curl-and-run bootstrap, no repo checkout needed
- dot_homebase/justfile: add update-nix-tools recipe (nix profile
  upgrade/add with JSON-based detection, macOS only, no-op on Linux)
- images/Containerfile: nix profile install → add, drop redundant
  experimental-features flag
- private_dot_ssh/config → config.tmpl: UseKeychain yes on macOS
- dot_zprofile: chezmoi drift check on all platforms (was Linux-only)
- homebase.toml: remove emacs from macOS casks (nix provides it)
- BOOTSTRAP.md: comprehensive rewrite covering both platforms
- bootstrap/bazzite.sh: pre-seed chezmoi vars, ssh-keyscan dedup
- dot_config/starship.toml: resolve merge conflict

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: parse cognito:groups bracket string from API Gateway JWT claims

API Gateway HTTP API serializes the JSON array ["Admins"] as the
literal string "[Admins]". The existing str->list conversion produced
["[Admins]"], so "Admins" not in ["[Admins]"] was always True -> 403.
Strip brackets and split on comma instead.

docs(claude-md): update workflow commands and file output rules

system (/plan:decompose, /plan:epic, /plan:spec, /plan:stories,
/workflow:run) — the old /workflow:decompose, /workflow:epic,
/workflow:story, /workflow:specify names no longer exist.

reference to avoid maintaining a duplicate list in CLAUDE.md.

PGCRYPTO_KEY Docker secret, so contributors know the required env
vars before first run.

tasks are tracked as GitHub Issues (not /tasks/ files), and state the
date format convention for plan filenames.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Simplify experiments page to two coming soon tiles

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix silent visit sync failure and add re-sync on page load

The submarket column was missing from Supabase, causing saveVisitToSupabase
to fail silently when visits had a submarket value set. This meant visits
were saved to localStorage but never synced to the cloud.

Fixes:
- Expanded column error retry to handle "does not exist" errors (not just
  schema cache misses), stripping unknown columns and retrying
- Added re-sync on page load: detects local-only visits that aren't in
  Supabase and pushes them automatically
- Added migration SQL for the missing submarket column

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(model): add tax return fact keys to INCOME_MAP

IRS extractors write TOTAL_INCOME, ORDINARY_BUSINESS_INCOME,
INTEREST_EXPENSE, and TAXABLE_INCOME — these fell through the
model builder producing null spread values.

Primary mappings (last-wins): INTEREST_EXPENSE→interest,
ORDINARY_BUSINESS_INCOME→netIncome.

Fallback mappings (first-wins): TOTAL_INCOME→revenue (only if
GROSS_RECEIPTS absent), TAXABLE_INCOME→netIncome (only if OBI absent).

commit.message:
docs(edge-cases): defer SA-034 (SSO out of scope); update PRD v2.0 refs

SA-034 (SSO Token Expiration During Long Recalculation) is explicitly
out of scope for v1: SSO/SAML/OIDC is deferred per the API contract.
Add a DEFERRED notice at the top of the SA-034 section and annotate
the summary table to prevent wasted implementation effort.

Update all three edge case documents and DELIVERY_SUMMARY.txt to
reference PRD v2.0 instead of v1.0 so reviewers know which version of
the requirements they should check edge cases against.

fix: use base + prod compose files in deploy script

The deploy script was using docker-compose.prod.yml alone, but that
file is an overlay that only adds volumes and overrides. The postgres
service gets its image (postgres:17-alpine) from the base
docker-compose.yml, so both files must be specified.

commit.message:
docs(prd): harden version deletion policy; fix document self-references

FR-VER-002: clarify that Published, Locked, and Archived versions are
immutable financial records and cannot be deleted (only Draft versions
can be deleted). The previous wording implied Published/Locked required
confirmation, which left ambiguity about Archived status and opened a
door for irreversible data loss.

FR-EXP-02: align PDF page size with ADR-014 (A3 landscape for P&L).

Section 19.3: correct the version control file path from the stale v1.0
path to docs/prd/BudFin_PRD_v2.0.md.

Acceptance criteria summary (§20): update FR-VER-002 entry to match
the hardened deletion policy.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

The PDF was untracked in assets/; moved to files/ (the conventional

[pn-e36942af] Add PROMPT_FILES env var resolution to ralph

Read PROMPT_FILES env var (colon-separated file list), expand $HOME and
~ in paths, resolve ./ relative to cwd, warn on missing files (non-fatal),
warn if env var is not set and fall back to default. Collected paths are
injected as --append-system-prompt-file args before --spec and --system-file
entries.

commit.message:
docs(ui-ux): introduce dual-shell architecture and workspace philosophy

Add 00b-workspace-philosophy.md — the foundational design rationale
for BudFin's two-shell approach (PlanningShell vs ManagementShell),
establishing when each is used and why.

Overhaul 00-global-framework.md:
- Define PlanningShell (context bar + docked right panel) and
  ManagementShell (sidebar + toolbar only, no context bar) with route
  tables and ASCII layout diagrams for both.
- Document auto-collapse behaviour (sidebar ↔ right panel interaction,
  comparison toggle, scenario overlay mode).
- Add React Router structure as an implementation reference.
- Reclassify context bar as PlanningShell-only; update layout from
  two-zone to three-zone (left/center/right).
- Add breadcrumb (3.1.0) and right panel toggle (3.1.8) elements.
- Fix WCAG AA contrast tokens: --status-draft → gray-600,
  --status-archived → gray-500.
- Add default inline error messages for all cell editor types.

Update module-specific specs (01–10) to reference the correct shell
for each module and align component tables with shadcn/ui primitives.

Use --no-start-daemon and disable sandbox for containers without systemd

In Docker containers lacking systemd, use the linux subcommand with
--no-start-daemon and --extra-conf "sandbox = false" to allow Nix
to install and run without a daemon or sandboxing.

Add method strength/weakness examples to alignment comparison article

Five targeted scenarios showing when each alignment method is the best
fit: smooth warping (elastic), feature shifts (landmark), noisy data
(smooth first), variable features (elastic), and ECG-like data
(constrained). Each includes code, plots, and practical guidance.

commit.message:
Add full scraping flow to AI search pipeline test

Background scrape runs on every query (matching production prescrape):
- Single company: 2 Serper pages per search
- Multi-company with 10+ results: skip (DB already rich)
- Multi-company with <10 results: 1 page per company, max 5

Flow: Serper discovery → filter known URLs → snippet pre-filter →
Apify scrape → save profiles → update scrape progress → re-query DB.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

docs(tdd): align with ADRs — 3-container stack, A3 PDF, new API endpoints

Cascade recent ADR decisions through all TDD sections:

- ADR-014 (PDF): correct page size from A4 landscape to A3 landscape
  for P&L reports across 01_overview, 02_component_design, and
  04_api_contract (FR-EXP-02).

- ADR-015 (workers): replace the four-container description with the
  canonical three-container stack (nginx, api with in-process pg-boss,
  db) in 01_overview infrastructure paragraph.

- ADR-017 (Grafana): note Grafana dashboard deferred to v2 in the
  observability paragraph of 01_overview.

- 02_component_design: add duplicate employee detection rules per
  PRD §16.1 (PO-003) — fuzzy 2-of-3 match criteria, confirmation
  dialog behavior, and audit entry spec. Correct import API routes
  to versioned paths (POST /api/v1/versions/:id/import/:module/validate
  and /commit with validationToken).

- 04_api_contract: add GET /api/v1/context endpoint (user identity +
  school year + permissions on app load). Add POST
  /api/v1/versions/:id/calculations/run (async pg-boss job trigger,
  202 response) and GET /api/v1/versions/:id/calculate/status
  (polling endpoint).

overlay functions.

commit.message:
fix: add missing AIOrchestrator import in draft_generator

The _generate_draft_tasks and _generate_draft_stories methods used
AIOrchestrator but it was never imported, causing NameError and falling
back to the legacy fallback method that generates generic cards without
dedup. This was the root cause of all duplicate generation issues.

commit.message:
admin: replace LLM model text input with fetch-from-server dropdown

Instead of typing the model name manually, the LLM server form now has
a "Fetch Models" button that queries the configured base URL (via the
existing test/ollama endpoint) and populates a dropdown with all
available models. If the fetch hasn't run yet, it falls back to a text
input so a model can still be entered manually.

An optional "Models endpoint" input is shown below (placeholder
"/api/tags" for Ollama) so other LLM servers with different list paths
can also be supported.
